title: The Hen With The Golden Eggs.
content:
To this lesson in greed,
    Pray, little ones, heed:

    Each day, we are told,
    A most wonderful Hen
    Laid an egg made of gold
    For this meanest of men.

    So greedy was he,
    He was not satisfied.
    "What is one egg to me?
    I want all that' inside!"

    He cut off her head,
    And began to explore.
    But the poor hen was dead.
    And could lay eggs no more.
meaning:
"The Hen With The Golden Eggs." is a quintessential example of Jean de La Fontaine's signature style...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
